warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.claim_text: Commutation ticket ridership to/from Manhattan is 41.5% lower than in 1990. Non-Commutation ticket ridership to/from Manhattan is 122.1% higher than in 1990. Intermediate ridership has increased by 91.0% compared to 1990.
payload.subject: Commutation vs Non-Commutation ridership trends

payload.claim_text: All these ridership trend percentages vs. 1990 reflect the impact of COVID and changing ticket-buying and ridership patterns, with telework policies making commutation tickets less attractive for those riders who have more flexible office days.
payload.subject: Telework impact on commutation

tool_args.label: Commutation share of East of Hudson ridership decline

payload.claim_text: Traditionally around half of MNR's total East of Hudson rail ridership for the past 20 pre-Covid years, Commutation in 2024 was 31% of East of Hudson ridership, compared to 62% in 1990.
payload.subject: Commutation share decline

payload.claim_text: Non-Commutation ticket ridership to and from Manhattan using One Way and Ten-Trip tickets was 122% higher than 1990 levels, when it represented approximately 26% of Metro-North ridership; this share increased to approximately 50% of total ridership for 2024.
payload.subject: Non-Commutation share increase

payload.claim_text: Intermediate ridership was 91% higher in 2024 than in 1990, representing 18% of total East of Hudson rail ridership, 2% above its pre-pandemic share of 16%.
payload.subject: Intermediate ridership share

payload.claim_text: This expansion continues to outpace the typical annual increases in ridership experienced in the decade before the pandemic and it is reasonable to expect continued ridership recovery in 2025 on all East of Hudson lines.
payload.subject: 2025 ridership outlook

tool_args.label: New Canaan Branch 2023 summer outage impact

payload.claim_text: The increase in New Canaan Branch ridership reflects ridership growth seen across the railroad as well as the difference between a full year of service in 2024 and the impact in 2023 of the summer maintenance service outage from May 30th to September 2nd.
payload.subject: New Canaan Branch 2023 outage

payload.gap_text: Ridership is calculated based on the number of tickets sold multiplied by a factor related to each different type of ticket. The Monthly/Weekly factor is based on the number of weekdays and weekend days per month.
payload.description: Ridership figures are estimates based on ticket sales multiplied by factors, not actual passenger counts.
